plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Sell semiconductor ETF (SMH) to lower sector concentration.
Trim concentrated positions (CRWD, SPY) to meet single-name limits.
Buy financial ETF (XLF) for diversification.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Hold a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Trim leveraged ETF (SOXL) to a small allocation.
Reduce exposure to semiconductors (SMH) to manage sector risk.
Trim concentrated positions (SPY, CRWD) to meet mandate limits.
Buy financial ETF (XLF) for diversification and add software name (PANW).
Increase income ETF (QQQI) allocation for stability.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer while balancing growth and risk.
balanced.read: This plan aims for balanced growth with a target of around 2.5% monthly return. It reduces leverage and concentration, blending broad market, quality tech, and income with a small allocation to leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell the lowest scoring leveraged ETF (SPAL) to reallocate.
Trim income (QQQI) and broad index (SPY) to fund growth areas.
Increase high-conviction tech (CRWD) and add leveraged semiconductor ETF (SOXL).
Buy high-scoring inverse ETF (TECS) and software name (PANW) for diversification.
Concentrate capital in top-scoring, higher-risk opportunities.
Maintain a small cash buffer, accepting higher potential drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly return by leaning into leverage and concentration. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risks of leveraged ETF decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Sell volatile single names (CRWD) and sector ETFs (SMH).
Trim broad index (SPY) slightly to fund income focus.
Maximize allocation to income ETF (QQQI).
Add financial ETF (XLF) for diversified income potential.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions and low drawdowns over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through distributions. It shifts capital away from growth and volatility towards income-generating ETFs, aiming for maximum stability and reliable cash flow.
